app.title: SelfClaude
app.description: Two-Claude orchestration: supervisor + developer with you in the loop
home.caption: multi-agent orchestration
home.hero.description: A supervisor + specialist agents working in parallel — you stay in the loop, gating phases and verifying work.
home.demo.title: 5-minute demo
home.demo.description: Watch the supervisor delegate to the developer to build a tiny portfolio HTML file. No prompt to write, no setup. End of run: one-click open in your browser.
home.realProject.title: Real project
home.realProject.description: Already have a folder you want to work on? Pick it and the wizard will set up SelfClaude in your project — fresh build or discover-existing.
home.baseline.tooltip: Multiplier used to estimate time saved on pinned project cards. The number is configurable and explicitly labelled — it's NOT a measured value, just a comparison against your chosen baseline.
agentPane.tab.notActive.title: {agent} — not active. Click to propose dispatching to sup.
agentPane.propose.tellSup: Tell sup what you'd like {label} to do.
agentPane.propose.detail: Sup will read your request, decide whether dispatching {label} is the right move (or push back if a different specialist fits better), and brief them with the context they need. You'll see sup's response in the supervisor chat on the left.
agentPane.propose.placeholder: e.g. "Please have {label} review the login form for accessibility issues."
agentPane.desc.developer: Backend / general-purpose implementation. Writes code, runs tests, edits configs.
agentPane.desc.uiDev: Frontend specialist (admin-panel oriented). shadcn/ui + Tailwind, strict standards.
agentPane.desc.security: Read-only auditor. Inspects diffs/configs for secrets, injection, auth bypass, deps.
agentPane.desc.tester: Verification-only specialist. Adds + runs tests; refuses to edit product code or change a passing assertion.
agentPane.desc.refactorer: Bounded-scope rework. Renames / splits / dedupes / tightens types; refuses features, new deps, or behaviour changes.
agentPane.desc.custom: Custom specialist. Sup will brief based on your request.
agentStatus.stop.title: emergency stop {role}
agentStatus.confirm.title: Stop the {role}?
agentStatus.confirm.message:
This will SIGTERM the {variantLabel}'s Claude Code subprocess immediately. Any in-flight tool call will be killed and the turn will end with an error. The session itself stays alive — you can prompt it again afterward.

Use this when the {variantLabel} appears stuck (a hung Bash command, a runaway loop, a wakeup that shouldn't have fired).
agentTimeline.empty.security: No audit reports yet. Talk to security directly via the input below, or wait for the supervisor to delegate a review.
agentTimeline.empty.agent: {agent} hasn't received a task yet. Prompt the agent directly via the input below, or let the supervisor delegate.
approvalDialog.title: Approval requested
approvalDialog.alwaysAllow: Always allow approvals for this session
approvalDialog.alwaysAllowWarning: ⚠ destructive ops (rm -rf, force push, drop table) will run without confirmation. Toggle off any time.

bottomToolbar.lastTurn.title: duration of the last completed role turn
bottomToolbar.cost.title: cumulative cost (sup + dev) in USD
bottomToolbar.turns.title: cumulative supervisor + developer turns
bottomToolbar.breakdown.title: per-role metrics breakdown
bottomToolbar.metrics.heading: metrics by role
bottomToolbar.failureBadge.none.title: No classified failures this session — Phase 7 catalogue.
bottomToolbar.contractBadge.none.title: No phase docs validated this session yet

bottomToolbar.contractBadge.title: Phase-contract first-pass rate: {pct}% ({distinctFilenames} doc(s) validated, {totalAttempts} total attempt(s), {overrides} override(s))

idleToast.title: All agents idle
infoBanners.chrome.title: Claude in Chrome
infoBanners.chrome.body: The supervisor can verify UI work + browse pages if you've installed {link} and granted permissions. Specialists don't get Chrome — that's intentional, sup holds the verification tool. (We can't detect whether the extension is installed, so this is just FYI.)
infoBanners.telegram.title: Telegram fallback
infoBanners.telegram.body: Questions or approvals you don't answer in 15 seconds can be forwarded to a Telegram chat. Set up a bot via {link}, put the token in {envFile}, then run {command} to pair the chat.
inputBar.placeholder.pendingApproval: y to allow, anything else denies ↵
isolationWidget.drift.label: isolation drift
isolationWidget.active.title: Isolation active on {branch} (forked from {originalBranch}). {commitCount} commit(s), {filesChanged} file(s) changed.
isolationWidget.accept.title: Squash-merge {branch} into {originalBranch}
isolationWidget.discard.title: Discard {branch} entirely (irreversible)
isolationWidget.isolate.title: fork a session branch off {currentBranch} so this run is reversible
isolationWidget.error.dirty: commit or stash your pending changes first — isolation refuses to fork off a dirty tree
isolationWidget.error.detachedHead: check out a branch first (HEAD is detached)
isolationWidget.dialog.accept.title: Squash-merge into {originalBranch}?
isolationWidget.dialog.accept.message:
Collapse {commitCount} commit(s) on {branch} into ONE squash commit on {originalBranch}, then delete {branch}.

The per-turn granular history disappears — be sure first.
isolationWidget.dialog.discard.title: Discard {branch}?
isolationWidget.dialog.discard.message:
Every commit on the branch + any uncommitted changes will be wiped. Worktree returns to {originalBranch}'s HEAD.

There is no undo.
wizard.title: New project setup
wizard.step1.heading: Step 1 — Project basics
wizard.step1.subtitle: What are we building? Type drives whether the supervisor applies a DNA template to the right specialist.
wizard.step1.projectName.label: Project name
wizard.step1.projectType.label: Project type
wizard.step1.goal.label: Goal (one line)
wizard.step1.goal.placeholder: e.g. Internal CRM for sales team to track leads + deals
wizard.step2.heading: Step 2 — Tech stack brief
wizard.step2.subtitle: Free-form paragraph. The supervisor parses this into a normalized {stackFile} (e.g. "next.js" / "nextjs" / "Next js" all become Next.js), locks the items you committed to, and reads it before delegating tasks. You can edit/lock individual items afterwards via the Stack panel.
wizard.step2.stackDescription.label: Stack description
wizard.step2.stackDescription.hint: Min 20 chars. Mention language, framework(s), database, key libraries — natural prose is fine.
wizard.step3.heading: Step 3 — Constraints (optional)
wizard.step3.subtitle: Hard requirements, things-not-to-touch, integrations the agents should honour. Anything that isn't a stack item but matters. Skip this step if you don't have anything to add — sup will ask follow-ups during Discovery.
wizard.step3.constraints.label: Constraints / non-goals / integrations
wizard.step3.constraints.hint: One per line works well. Or free prose. Empty is fine.
wizard.step4.heading: Step 4 — Review
wizard.step4.subtitle: Confirm and launch. The supervisor will use this as authoritative input — parse the stack brief into a normalized manifest, apply DNA if applicable, write CLAUDE.md, then ask follow-up questions for anything missing.
wizard.footer.skipWizard.idle: skip wizard, talk to sup directly
wizard.discover.title: Already-built project?
wizard.discover.body: If this folder already contains code, skip the wizard — sup will read the manifests, map the architecture, and ask what you want to work on.
wizard.projectType.adminPanel.label: Admin panel / dashboard
wizard.projectType.adminPanel.descriptio: CRUD tables, modals, sidebar+header layout. Triggers admin-panel DNA on ui-dev.
wizard.projectType.marketingSite.label: Marketing site / landing
wizard.projectType.marketingSite.descrip: Bespoke design, mostly content + animation. No DNA applied; ui-dev stays general.
wizard.projectType.library.label: Library / package
wizard.projectType.library.description: Reusable code with no UI; tests + types are the deliverable.
wizard.projectType.mobile.label: Mobile app
wizard.projectType.mobile.description: iOS / Android / cross-platform. No bundled DNA yet; ui-dev gets general guidance.
wizard.projectType.other.label: Other
wizard.projectType.other.description: CLI, daemon, ML pipeline — anything else. Sup will ask follow-ups.
scriptProposal.title: Script proposal — needs your review
scriptProposal.dismiss.title: Review later (Esc)
scriptProposal.rejectForm.placeholder: Why are you rejecting? Sup will see this verbatim.
scriptProposal.error.rejectRequiresReaso: Reject requires a reason — sup needs to know what to fix.

sessionsList.metrics.files.tooltip: distinct files touched across every session in this project
sessionsList.metrics.firstPass.tooltip: phase-contract first-pass rate across this project — {docCount} doc(s), {totalAttempts} total attempts, {overrides} overrides
sessionsList.metrics.estimate.tooltip: Estimate ({baseline}× baseline). Active {activeDuration} actual. At {baseline}× the manual baseline would have implied {totalDuration} — delta ≈ {delta} saved. Configure baseline in Settings.
settings.title: Settings
settings.tab.mcpTools.disabled: Open a session to view MCP telemetry
settings.prompts.resetButton.title: delete the override and revert to bundled default
settings.prompts.emptyState: Select an agent on the left to edit its system prompt.
settings.prompts.badge.custom.title: custom override saved
settings.prompts.badge.unsaved.title: unsaved changes
settings.prompts.confirmReset: Reset {label} to bundled default? This deletes your override.
settings.mcp.emptyState: Select a tool on the left to see its usage detail.
settings.mcp.noCalls.hint: If sup should be using this tool but isn't, it's a signal the prompt directive isn't landing — open the Agent prompts tab to inspect / tune the relevant system prompt.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.askUser: Ask the operator a clarifying question. Bundle multiple as a numbered list in one call.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.requestApp: Request operator approval before destructive / scope-changing actions.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.writePhase: Write a phase brief into docs/phases/.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.registerPh: Sup-only. Declare the DoD checklist for a phase (Documentation step).
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.proposeIte: Any agent. Mark a tracker item as proposed-done; sup reviews next turn.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.confirmIte: Sup-only. Confirm a proposed item after verification.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.rejectItem: Sup-only. Send a proposed item back to pending with a reason.
settings.mcp.toolDescriptions.applyAgent: Sup-only. Apply a bundled DNA template to a specific agent.
statusBar.backToHome.title: Back to home
statusBar.demo.title: Demo ready — open the generated index.html in your browser
stuckBanner.title: Session looks stuck
stuckBanner.ageLabel.withMinutes: {minutes} min since the last file change or decision
stuckBanner.body.noProgressYet: The supervisor has run several turns without delegating any writes or making a phase-tracker decision. Often this means sup is stuck in discussion — try sending it a more directive message, or prompt the developer/specialists directly via their tab.
stuckBanner.body.alive: {ageLabel}. The session is alive but isn't moving forward; either an agent is looping on something or sup is waiting for an instruction it didn't ask for. Send a follow-up prompt, click Stop on the active turn, or wait if you know it's working in the background.
supChat.empty: No messages yet. Type below to start a discovery conversation.
supChat.copy.title: copy message text
tabBar.home.title: SelfClaude home
tabBar.close.title: Close {label}
toolCard.elapsed.title: elapsed since dispatch
failureMode.toolError.label: tool error
failureMode.toolError.description: A tool call (Edit / Write / Bash / Read / etc.) failed during an agent turn. The tool returned a non-success result; the agent saw the error in its context and may retry on its next turn.
failureMode.toolError.suggestedAction: Open the tool detail pane to see the underlying error. Common causes: missing file, permission denied, syntax issue in the input. The agent typically recovers on the next turn — intervene only if it loops.
failureMode.agentTimeout.label: agent timeout
failureMode.agentTimeout.description: The agent subprocess did not complete its turn within the orchestrator-allowed window. The turn was terminated; partial output may have landed.
failureMode.agentTimeout.suggestedAction: Send the agent a follow-up message asking it to break the work into smaller turns. If the timeout repeats, restart the session — long-running CC sessions sometimes accumulate state that slows them down.
failureMode.contextOverflow.label: context overflow
failureMode.contextOverflow.description: The agent hit the model context limit. CC dropped older history to fit the remaining work, but cumulative context is still tight.
failureMode.contextOverflow.suggestedAct: Wrap up the current phase and start a new session against the same project. The chat-log + state.json will be replayed; the operating context resets cleanly.
failureMode.hookValidation.label: hook denied
failureMode.hookValidation.description: A SelfClaude pre-tool hook (destructive-action gate, file-lock, phase-contract) rejected the agent's tool call. The denial is intentional — the orchestrator caught a contract violation before it touched disk.
failureMode.hookValidation.suggestedActi: Read the rejection message carefully. The contract is doing its job; either approve the action via the operator dialog or guide the agent to a different approach.
failureMode.networkError.label: network error
failureMode.networkError.description: A network call failed mid-turn (Anthropic API, MCP bridge, hook server). Usually transient; the orchestrator retries where it can.
failureMode.networkError.suggestedAction: Wait a beat and let the retry logic do its job. If it persists, check your internet connection and the Anthropic status page. Hard-failed turns are safe to re-run — chat-log + git branch capture all work to date.
failureMode.mcpCrash.label: MCP bridge error
failureMode.mcpCrash.description: The MCP bridge between the agent and the orchestrator returned a non-success. Common when the orchestrator endpoint validates its inputs (zod schema rejection).
failureMode.mcpCrash.suggestedAction: Check the agent's most recent tool call — the rejection message is in the tool result. Often the agent recovers automatically on the next turn.
failureMode.agentAborted.label: aborted
failureMode.agentAborted.description: The agent turn was aborted, usually by the operator clicking the stop button or closing the session mid-flight. Any in-flight work is captured in chat-log up to the abort point.
failureMode.agentAborted.suggestedAction: No action needed if you intended this. The chat-log preserves what landed; the next turn picks up cleanly.
failureMode.unknown.label: unknown error
failureMode.unknown.description: A failure surfaced that doesn't map to any catalogued mode. The orchestrator log has the underlying message; consider filing this as a triage item so the catalog can grow.
failureMode.unknown.suggestedAction: Open the audit log for the timestamped error message. If the same error recurs, open an issue with the message + steps so we can extend the catalog.

rightSidebar.toolDetail.empty: Click a tool call in the timeline to see its full input and result here.
rightSidebar.tasks.noList: No task list yet (developer hasn't called TodoWrite).
rightSidebar.schedule.empty: Nothing scheduled — no wakeups, no cron jobs.
rightSidebar.phases.empty.heading: .selfclaude/phases.json
rightSidebar.phases.empty.body: The supervisor populates the phase tracker during Documentation by calling register_phase_items for each phase (matching docs/phases/*.md briefs). Once that fires, items appear here with live status — ⚪ pending · 🟡 proposed · ✅ done.
rightSidebar.phases.unverified.title: {unverified} done item(s) confirmed without verification — needs operator review
rightSidebar.phases.proposed.title: {proposed} item awaiting supervisor review
rightSidebar.phases.emptyConfirm.heading: Confirmed without verification evidence
rightSidebar.phases.emptyConfirm.body: Supervisor confirmed this item without making any Read, Bash, or Edit call between proposal and confirmation. To clear this warning:
rightSidebar.phases.emptyConfirm.askSup: Ask sup to re-review in chat — the next confirm captures a fresh trail.
rightSidebar.phases.emptyConfirm.markVer: Mark operator-verified if you've inspected the work yourself.
rightSidebar.phases.emptyAuditTrail: ⚠ Empty audit trail. No Read / Bash / Edit recorded between propose and confirm. The supervisor may have rubber-stamped this item — push back if it matters.
rightSidebar.audit.empty.heading: audit log empty
rightSidebar.audit.empty.body: Every phase tracker mutation lands here: register_phase_items · propose_item_done · confirm_item_done · reject_item_done. Drive-by confirms with empty evidence trails get flagged ⚠.
rightSidebar.audit.detail.opVerifiedBy: Operator-verified by {operator} — manual override clearing the empty-evidence ⚠.
rightSidebar.audit.detail.rubberStamp: Supervisor confirmed without recording any Read / Bash / Edit tool call between proposal and confirmation. Open the phases panel to operator-verify or request re-review.
rightSidebar.memory.section.projectRules: No CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md yet. The supervisor writes these during bootstrap; you can also create them manually.
rightSidebar.memory.section.sharedMemory: No shared memory yet. Sup writes here for cross-agent durable notes.
rightSidebar.memory.section.ccAutoMemory: No CC auto-memory files for this project yet. Sup writes here when the operator says 'add to memory'.
rightSidebar.decisions.empty: No decisions yet. Verdicts, phase confirmations, approval decisions, and task delegations land here as they happen — every binding moment in the project, chronological.
rightSidebar.decisions.noEntries: No entries in this category yet.
rightSidebar.decisions.export.title: Export the decision trail as a shareable markdown file
rightSidebar.room.empty: No chat yet. Specialists post here via <ROOM>…</ROOM> when they need to coordinate with each other. Every message forwards to the supervisor (the moderator); sup-decided outcomes show up in the Decision Room tab as numbered verdicts.
rightSidebar.stack.lock.title: lock — agents must honour
rightSidebar.stack.unlock.title: unlock — agents may suggest changes
rightSidebar.stack.remove.title: remove
rightSidebar.stack.summary.title: Stack summary
rightSidebar.stack.summary.subtitle: Plain-language readout of the tech manifest.
rightSidebar.stack.summary.copy.title: Copy summary to clipboard
rightSidebar.stack.summary.empty: No values filled in yet. Open a category and pick the stack choices the project will use.
rightSidebar.scripts.emptyBody: When the supervisor finds itself running the same Bash command repeatedly, it can call propose_script to suggest a reusable shell script. You'll see the proposal here with the body + reason — review and approve to add it to .selfclaude/scripts/, or reject with a reason. Sup calls approved scripts via the regular Bash tool.
Why are you rejecting? Sup will see this verbatim.
